Security Model

Comprehensive security architecture and data protection

Critical
Security Principles
1

Encrypt Secrets

All API keys encrypted at rest (AES-256)

2

HTTPS Everywhere

TLS 1.3 for all communications

3

JWT Authentication

Short-lived tokens with refresh rotation

4

Role Based Access

RBAC with principle of least privilege

5

Rotate Secrets

Automatic key rotation every 90 days

6

No Secrets in Browser

Credentials never exposed to frontend

Encryption Architecture

At Rest

  • • Database: AES-256 encryption
  • • API Keys: Encrypted columns in Supabase
  • • Backups: Encrypted S3 storage
  • • Keys: AWS KMS / HashiCorp Vault

In Transit

  • • TLS 1.3 for all connections
  • • Certificate pinning for Workers
  • • mTLS between internal services
  • • VPN for VPS management
Authentication Flow
// JWT Token Structure
interface JWTPayload {
  sub: string;        // user_id
  email: string;
  role: "user" | "admin";
  plan: string;
  iat: number;        // issued at
  exp: number;        // expires (1 hour)
}

// Refresh Token
interface RefreshPayload {
  sub: string;
  token_version: number;
  exp: number;        // expires (7 days)
}

// Row Level Security
policies: {
  "users can only read own data":
    auth.uid() = user_id,
  "admins can read all":
    auth.jwt()->>'role' = 'admin'
}
Execution Environment Isolation

VPS Isolation

Each project has dedicated Hetzner VPS

Docker Containers

Worker runs in isolated container

Network Segmentation

Private networks per customer

Credential Isolation

No shared secrets between projects

Audit Logging
// All security events logged
interface AuditEvent {
  id: uuid;
  user_id: string;
  action: string;
  resource: string;
  resource_id: string;
  changes: JSON;
  ip_address: string;
  user_agent: string;
  timestamp: DateTime;
}

// Events tracked:
// - Login/logout
// - API key creation/deletion
// - Project creation/deletion
// - Credential access
// - Billing changes
// - Permission changes
